exec_summary.subtitle: -69% from peak  ·  3 services left today  ·  FrenchFines is the entire surface after EOD
headline_bullets: Criticals concentrated on 3 services — FrenchFines (268), tariffmod-facade (3), sss-dbinit (2)
tariffmod-facade + sss-dbinit closing today; FrenchFines becomes 100% of remaining surface
ES production data migration complete across AU/EU/US — unblocks Control + FrenchFines

dependency_grid.title: ES production data migration
dependency_grid.narrative: Operational prerequisite for both Control and FrenchFines upgrades. Now complete across all regions.

rationale_bullets: Node 8 on Debian Buster — 5+ years past EOL for both runtime and OS.
After today's fixes on tariffmod-facade + sss-dbinit, 100% of BRM criticals sit here.
ES production data migration is now complete — the only remaining dependency is in hand.
Late June target close aligned with PSDEV-62502 epic.

epic_status.summary: Security vulnerability tasks. Today's tariffmod-facade + sss-dbinit fixes ship under this epic. Primary burndown tracker.
Control technical upgrade. Merged to main. ES production migration complete. Deploys 2026-06-03.
FrenchFines technical upgrade. 5/10 sub-tasks complete. Closes the tail late June.

asks.title: Acknowledge the concentration
asks.body: After today, 100% of remaining BRM criticals sit on FrenchFines. Clean shape for the next 5 weeks.

asks.title: Endorse the resource reallocation
asks.body: Still in force; Pulse + Datadog monitoring streams absorbing the cost through Q2.

asks.title: Confirm criticality vs. roadmap
asks.body: These vulnerabilities remain the highest priority over Pulse self-service rate management for BRM through Q2.
